productIdea: An inventory planning workspace for a small business that tracks stock states, thresholds, and adjustments without adding unnecessary ERP complexity.
targetAudience: Store managers, stock operators, and owners reviewing low-stock decisions.
problemStatement: Small businesses often track inventory inconsistently, which causes stock surprises, unclear adjustments, and reactive ordering.
constraints: Keep the first release simple, local-first, and focused on stock-state clarity, low-stock thresholds, and adjustment review.
desiredOutput: A build-ready markdown workspace with phased planning, stock state definitions, threshold rules, gates, verification files, handoff context, and enough clarity for an inventory tracking tool.
mustHaveFeatures: Inventory items, stock states, low-stock thresholds, adjustment notes, review workflow, purchase-plan deferrals.
niceToHaveFeatures: Supplier tracking, purchase order generation, barcode scanning, multi-location support.
dataAndIntegrations: Inventory items, stock counts, threshold values, adjustment history, purchase-plan notes.
risks: Low-stock thresholds may be unclear, adjustments may not be trustworthy, and the package could drift into irrelevant finance or clinic concerns.
successMetrics: A reviewer can trace low-stock, adjustment, and deferred purchase planning behavior with clear evidence.
nonGoals: No accounting suite, no POS integrations, no warehouse robotics.
timeline: Plan a realistic first release that proves the inventory workflow works before adding richer features.
teamContext: A store manager uses MVP Builder to prepare the inventory plan and then hands the workspace to Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or a development team for phased implementation.

questionnaireAnswers.north-star: The first release must make stock states, low-stock thresholds, and adjustments explicit enough to guide implementation.
questionnaireAnswers.primary-workflow: A manager reviews stock states, records adjustments, checks low-stock items, and decides what purchase planning stays deferred.
questionnaireAnswers.data-boundaries: Important boundaries include item records, threshold fields, adjustment history, and low-stock review notes.
questionnaireAnswers.scope-cut: Keep stock states, thresholds, and adjustment review in v1. Defer accounting, vendor automation, and multi-location complexity.
questionnaireAnswers.acceptance: A reviewer should be able to trace inventory state changes and understand which purchase behaviors are deferred.
questionnaireAnswers.operating-risks: The biggest risks are unclear thresholds, weak adjustment trust, and over-promising unsupported inventory features.
questionnaireAnswers.deployment-guardrails: Keep the artifacts local-first and require explicit notes when repo targets are assumptions.
questionnaireAnswers.test-proof: Reviewers should capture low-stock and adjustment scenarios with observed results before advancement.
